title: Control Flow Complexity of Methods
description: This rule raises an issue when the code cognitive complexity of a function is above a certain threshold. High cognitive complexity is a sign that the code should be refactored into smaller, easier-to-manage pieces.
recommendation: Refactor the method into smaller, easier-to-manage pieces.
impact: High cognitive complexity makes the code hard to read, understand, test, and modify.

checks.message: Method has high cognitive complexity with deeply nested control structures
